title: CHILD WELFARE SERVICES - 211info
text: DALLAS CHILD WELFARE AND SELF-SUFFICIENCY OFFICE CHILD WELFARE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help in keeping families united. Finds temporary and permanent homes for kids who need them, including foster and adoptive homes. The program also teaches people how to become resource parents. 190 W Ellendale Avenue Dallas, OR 97338 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm, closed on major holidays (855) 503-7233 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers State Child Abuse and Neglect Hotline (855) 503-7233 Main phone (503) 623-5526 Self-Sufficiency Main phone (503) 623-8118 Child Welfare INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for an appointment FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Polk County Services: Adoption Services Family Preservation Programs Foster Home Placement Foster Parent/Family Recruitment Other Services Offered At This Location: INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CHILD ABUSE REPORTING MUTUAL SUPPORT - FOSTER, ADOPT, AND KINSHIP PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - EMPLOYMENT RELATED DAYCARE PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - SNAP PROGRAM PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - SUMMER EBT PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - TEMPORARY ASSISTANCE FOR NEEDY FAMILIES PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - TEMPORARY ASSISTANCE TO DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SURVIVORS If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
